entry.title: Re: DMC:Value XX cannot be converted; integer part can ha...
entry.content:
Hello

To solve the issue with the conversion of the custom field, you may need to adjust the data mapping and formatting in the migration template. Here are some steps you can take to address the error message:

1. Check the data mapping in the migration template to ensure that the custom field is correctly defined with 3 integer and 2 decimal digits.

2. Verify that the values being migrated for the custom field adhere to the defined format of 3 integer and 2 decimal digits.

3. If the error continues, consider adjusting the format of the custom field in the migration template to accommodate values that may have more than 1 integer digit.

4. If the issue persists, you may need to review the data source and identify any discrepancies in the values being migrated for the custom field.

Please let me know if this works or not. 

Best Regards, 

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: E Mail Header and E mail Body not in proper format fo...
entry.content:
I would do the following.

Download the xml of an existing PO so you see the correct placeholder.

Based on that you can freely create your own email templates based on the xml

same for the email subject. I would do it with word as you can directly save in htm file extension.

 

Please also have a look into the help how the format should look like:

below a simple example of an htm email template for a customer invoice:

Dear Sir or Madam,

Good Day. Please find the [CustomerInvoice-ProcessingTypeName] [CustomerInvoice-ID] posted on [CustomerInvoice-date] for the amount [CustomerInvoice-PriceAndTax-GrossAmount] [CustomerInvoice-PricingTerms-CurrencyCode] attached.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Yours sincerely,

[CustomerInvoice-BillFromParty-FormattedName]

[CustomerInvoice-BillFromParty-FormattedAddressDescription]

Phone No.:- [CustomerInvoice-BillFromParty-FormattedConventionalPhoneNumberDescription]

Fax No. :- [CustomerInvoice-BillFromParty-FormattedFacsimileNumberDescription]

Email :- [CustomerInvoice-BillFromParty-EmailURI]

 

Best regards

 

Didi

entry.title: Re: Getting Error while activating the Agreement/B2b scenario in TPM in SAP CPI

entry.title: Managing Overtime Pre-approval in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Managing Overtime Pre-approval in SAP SuccessFactors

In the realm of HR management, especially in regions like the Middle East, handling overtime can often be a complex task. Recently, I encountered a requirement from a business where employees needed to obtain pre-approval for overtime before actually logging it. This challenge led me to explore various solutions, consulting experts like Neelesh Kamath, and ultimately devising an effective approach within SAP SuccessFactors.

On-Premise SAP Configuration

In an on-premise SAP environment, configuring such controls typically involves using IT2006 to create an overtime approval quota. This allows employees to apply for overtime in CATS / Fiori Time sheet only within the predefined date and time, ensuring compliance with company policies and labor regulations.

Transition to SuccessFactors Time Tracking

Moving to SAP SuccessFactors posed a new challenge as the direct equivalent of IT2006 and its strict time-bound controls was not available. However, through careful consideration and leveraging the platform's capabilities, I devised a structured solution that satisfied the client's requirements.

Solution Framework in SuccessFactors

  1. Creation of Attendance Types:
    • Pre-Approval Overtime: Under the Extra Category, an attendance type was created specifically for pre-approved overtime. This type was configured with the Single Record Approval feature.
    • Actual Overtime: Another attendance type was created under the Attendance category, designed for actual overtime logging without any approval requirement.
  2. Workflow Overview:
    • Employee Initiates Pre-Approval: Employees can apply in advance for pre-approved overtime using the designated attendance type. This request triggers a single record approval workflow, where the manager reviews and approves the request.

 

 

    • Impact on Timesheet Submission: Until the pre-approval is granted, the employee cannot submit their timesheet for approval, ensuring all overtime is sanctioned before being logged.
    • Logging Actual Overtime: On the day of the scheduled overtime, the employee logs the actual hours worked using the attendance type configured without approval.

 

    • Timesheet Submission: At the end of the week, the employee submits their timesheet containing both pre-approved and actual overtime details.

 

Bonus Features

  1. Integration with Time Sheet Approval Center:
    • I explored the new features of the Time Sheet Approval Center to enhance visibility and control over overtime entries. This centralizes approvals and provides comprehensive oversight for managers.

 

 

To enrich the user experience and ensure seamless implementation, I incorporated overtime details into key facts displayed within the Time Sheet Approval Center. This allowed for quick reference and streamlined approval processes.

 

Conclusion

Navigating the intricacies of overtime management between SAP on-premise and SuccessFactors requires a nuanced approach. By customizing attendance types and leveraging approval workflows, I successfully addressed the client's requirements while adhering to industry best practices and platform capabilities.

For detailed insights and further reference, you can explore the reference documents and community resources provided:

  • How to Setup Time Evaluation in Time Tracking
  • Time Sheet Approval Center

This solution not only met the immediate needs of the client but also set a robust framework for future enhancements and scalability within SAP SuccessFactors.

entry.title: Re: Spool file authority setting
entry.content:
Hello @rowag,

on my test system I don't have a VL74. I suggest to do a trace recording (e.g. via ST12) and have a look into the trace whether one or both of the function modules is called. Or maybe if there is another function module used for creating the spool output.

Kind regards

Jan

- SAP Event Broker

- SAP Event Mesh

- Event Mesh Integration Suite (EMIS, since this doesn't seem to be the same as "SAP Event Mesh"?)

- Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh (AEM)

entry.title: Re: Excel download in SAP Fiori Elements app gets stuck i...
entry.content:
• Set the newly added downloadLimit property on the dataSource configuration (OR)
  • Implement $top and $skip

This new behavior ensures that any additional data (since last table binding) is not ignored during export...

entry.title: Re: Edit a migration template
entry.content:
Hello,

To edit the migration template in the staging area in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, follow these steps:

1. Log in to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition account and navigate to the Migration Cockpit.

2. In the Migration Cockpit, select the migration object for which you want to edit the template.

3. In the staging area, click on the "Edit Template" button to open the migration template in edit mode.

4. Make the necessary changes to the template, such as adding or removing fields, changing field mappings, or updating data values.

5. Save the changes to the template.

As for the option for mass change for a particular field in the template in the staging area, it depends on the specific functionality and features available in the Migration Cockpit for the selected migration object. In some cases, there may be options to perform mass changes to a particular field using data manipulation functions or by applying global changes to data values.

It's important to note that the available options and functionalities may vary based on the specific version and configuration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that you are using. If you require specific guidance on performing mass changes for a particular field in a migration template, please let us know.

Best Regards,

Harish Mangtani

Elevate Efficiency with Automation Solutions at Amiseq

In the modern business landscape, automation solutions are pivotal in optimizing processes, enhancing productivity, and driving innovation. Amiseq specializes in delivering cutting-edge automation solutions tailored to meet your specific operational needs. From streamlining workflows to improving data management systems, Amiseq empowers businesses to achieve unparalleled efficiency and scalability.

Amiseq's automation consultancy services provide comprehensive support from initial assessment to implementation and beyond. Our consultants collaborate closely with your team to develop customized automation strategies that align with your business objectives and accelerate your digital transformation journey.

Discover how Amiseq can revolutionize your business operations with advanced automation solutions and expert consultancy. Transform your organization with our tailored approach to automation, ensuring sustainable growth and competitive advantage in today's fast-paced market.

entry.title: SAP Ariba: How to import Analytical Reports from Test to Production realms
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Often during SAP Ariba implementation, we create several Analytical Reports in Ariba Test realm as per customer's requirements. However, it becomes a redundant task to create those same reports in Production realm during cutover.

This blog explains the steps to import Analytical Reports from Ariba test realm to Production realm.

Pre-requisite:

Parameter Application.Analysis.ReportDumpFromUIEnabled must be set as Yes in Ariba Test and Production realms.

This parameter is controlled by SAP Ariba Support team and you need to create a case to set this parameter value as Yes.

Procedure:

Step 1: In your Test realm, move all the reports which you want to export in a new folder under ‘Public Reports’ folder

Step 2: Click on the folder 🡪 Click ‘Action’ under Create select Export Reports in Folder to File and select a destination to save in your local system and save the file.

Step 3: In your Production realm, under Public Reports create a folder in which you want to import the reports.

Step 4: Click on the folder 🡪 Click Action > under Create select Import Reports from File to Folder 🡪 Select the file you saved in your local system and then click Import

Following additional points needs to be ensured before importing the reports from one realm to another:

  • Ensure you have Full System Groups assigned for Report Administration. 
  • Ensure Reports Exported does not contain any Custom Fields, Custom Measures, and Custom Measure Calculations.
  • Avoid Reports with Fact Sources not available in Destination Realm.
  • Ensure Reports Exported does not contain Filter Criteria for selected values only existing in Source Realm
  • Avoid using Personal Workspace due to potential conflicts or limitations of User Roles, Permissions, or Folder Access.
  • Ensure Report UNIQUE Owner ID exist in both Source + Destination Realm. To ensure uniqueness, it is safe to have all Report Owner as functional Owner Username (Customer Support Admin). Owner of a Report Name can be changed under Attributes for a Report Name. Change to Owner (Customer Support Admin) requires this User to perform this.
  • Ensure the content in the Folder Exported ONLY contain plain SAP Ariba Reports (i.e. not other Document Formats).

 

SAP Ariba Sourcing SAP Ariba Spend Analysis SAP Ariba Procurement

entry.title: Re: Thoughts on SAP CodeConnect 2024
entry.content: Great summary and good thoughts about this year's conference(s)! I'm glad that you liked my session and maybe we can see each other next year on the conference 🙂

entry.title: Hierarchy Nodes Assignment in SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring is Now Available
entry.content:
Assign Hierarchy Nodes

You can now assign Hierarchy Nodes to Custom Solution Processes in SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring. To do this, go to the details view and select the relations tab.

 

 

Takeover of Hierarchy Nodes

Once the Custom Solution Process is published, it can be utilized in the Processes app. In the details view of the Custom Solution Process within this app, you will see the inherited Hierarchy Nodes, which cannot be changed unless you return to Process Authoring. However, you can still add new relevant hierarchy nodes in the Processes app.

 

Conclusion

Hierarchy Nodes is the initial relation available for Custom Solution Processes, with more relations expected to follow.

For more informations about the benefit from having Hierarchy Nodes in SAP Cloud ALM, please refer to this blog.

If you want to know more about SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring capabilities, please refer to the Walkthrough SAP Cloud ALM Process Authoring blog.

entry.title: Qm instection lot status "MCAN"

However, I have noticed a lot with the status "MCAN - Lot marked for cancellation" in my system. Can you explain how the system ends up with  the MCAN status for the lots?

This is for the lot with origin 89.

-

entry.title: Why should you watch the 'Back to Basics with SAP CAP' series on YouTube?
entry.content:
First, let me say a few words about the author and presenter of this series, DJ Adams. I must confess, I might be a bit biased since he is a fellow SAP colleague. However, my admiration is well-founded. I have immense respect for anyone who has the Unix console skills to type out HTTP requests with headers, hit enter, and make it work seamlessly while addressing questions in a live chat.

So why CAP and why watch yet another series on YouTube? I could just ask AI to do it for me, right?

SAP has strategically developed opinionated frameworks like CAP to empower developers with freedom rather than restriction. This YouTube series is a comprehensive resource that covers a wide array of topics essential for both novice and experienced developers while explaining CAP. It offers a step-by-step guide to quickly start with CAP and SQLite using VSCode and plugins, explains the nuances of dev and non-dev dependencies, and much more.

Example: Let's look at some of the key topics covered in the series:

  1. Quick Start with CAP and SQLite:
    • The series begins by demonstrating how to use VSCode and plugins to set up a local Docker environment with all dependencies. This quick start guide is essential for getting up and running with CAP in no time.
  2. Understanding Dependencies:
    • Explaining the difference between development and non-development dependencies, and detailing what package.json is and how it works, provides a deeper understanding of project configuration and management.
  3. CAP Internals:
    • How does CAP know what to do and when? By exploring commands like cds env get roots, the series reveals the inner workings of the CAP framework.
  4. Local Development Server:
    • Learn when and how to use cds watch to spin up a local server, and how to post requests, which is crucial for local development and testing.
  5. Entity Definitions and Aspects:
    • Decorating entity definitions with aspects enhances your data models, making them more robust and scalable.
  6. Build Profiles:
    • Building and triggering different build profiles helps in managing various stages of application development and deployment.
  7. Data Models:
    • Structuring data models with associations and compositions ensures that your applications are well-organized and maintainable.
  8. Modern JavaScript:
    • Topics like arrow functions and ES6 features, including classes and fat arrow functions, are covered to enhance your JavaScript skills, which are crucial for modern web development.

..and much much more. 

You can find all the episodes here:  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FxOQsg8yc2s&ab_channel=SAPDevelopers

Thanks!

Alex

entry.title: BTP Abap Environment additionalBinding metadata cache
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm developing a Fiori Elements app using RAP in the SAP BTP ABAP environment. This application includes Value Helps with additionalBinding. However, I have noticed that sometimes changes to the additionalBinding for a Value Help are not reflected on the frontend.

Upon investigation, I discovered that these Value Helps, which are based on other CDS Views, make a metadata call the first time they are invoked on the frontend.

This metadata, separate from the main Fiori Elements metadata, contains the additionalBinding as a Value Help input parameter. Unfortunately, it does not update according to the changes made in the CDS.

Eventually, the metadata updates after modifying the metadata extension object in other places. This issue is difficult to consistently replicate.

I suspect this might be a caching problem on the backend. In an on-premise system, we could clear the backend cache directly by running a report. However, in BTP, there seems to be no straightforward way to do this. How can I check if the cache is the problem, and how can I invalidate it to update this metadata on the frontend?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Check Clearing Date
entry.content:
Hello, 

To retrieve the "Check Clearing Date" field in a Custom CDS View in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can look into the standard CDS View FINS_ACDOCA.

The "ABLAD" (Clearing Date) field in this CDS View represents the "Check Clearing Date" that you are looking for. You can include this field in your custom payment report by enhancing the standard CDS View or creating a new custom CDS View based on FINS_ACDOCA.

I hope this helps. Good luck with your report customization!

Best Regards,
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Where can I learn more about SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Foundation Option?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Foundation Option learning journey learning journey

Overview

This learning journey covers the configuration and administration of SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Foundation Option.

Learning objectives

In this Learning Journey, you will learn about the configuration and administration of each of the modules in SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Foundation Option. Each of the base modules is covered. This learning journey is designed for consultants, technical leads, or anyone who may be involved in a Supply Chain Collaboration deployment or wants to understand the process to configuring and administrating each of the Supply Chain Collaboration modules in the Foundation Option.

Prerequisites

  • Discovering SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Foundation Option Features and Functions
  • Discovering SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration Add On Features and Functions

Book your certification exam
Validate your skills and learning by completing the certification exam and gain a highly recognised accreditation.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inital bank balance upload using memo record.
entry.content:
Hi, 

From the App Memo record 2.0 you can only create the initial balances for the bank account refer SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3452616/E 

Use the migration object FI - G/L account balance and open/line item help portal link for uploading the initial G/L Account balances.

 

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: How to Prepare Your Application for the Blockage of Third-Party Cookies.
entry.content:
Browsers Are Ending Third-Party Cookie Support

Browser manufacturers are ending support of third-party cookies. Google Chrome is the most prominent example, with its third-party cookie deprecation program planned for the end of 2024.

In our fourth and last installment of our blog series dedicated to the third-party cookie deprecation, we’ll glance over the definitive solutions you can implement for your application if it is affected: Cookies Having Independent State (CHIPS) and Storage Access API (SSA).

As a reminder, our previous blog covered the deployment of the deprecation trial token. If your solution is affected, you must get active and deploy your deprecation trial token by the end of the grace period.

If your custom domain was approved for a deprecation trial before July 1st 2024, the end of the grace period is set to August 30th, 2024.

If your custom domain was approved on July 1st or after, you have 60 days starting from the date your custom domain was approved to deploy your deprecation trial token.

Check out our blog and our dedicated step-by-step guide to get active now.

 

Implementing CHIPS or SAA

Before even considering CHIPS or SAA, make sure that you test your solution for breakage first. See our dedicated step-by-step guide for more information: Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation.

At SAP, we recommend implementing Cookies Having Independent State (CHIPS) or Storage Access API (SAA). Which solution you should apply depends entirely on your scenario and the context of your application. Both solutions have pros but also limitations you should be aware of. We’ve listed them in our full guide dedicated to these solutions.

SAP has made changes to Approuter and SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service to make them CHIPS and SAA ready out of the box.  These components use a combination of CHIPS and SAA to negate the effects of third-party cookie deprecation.

However, because SAP wants customers and partners to fully test their application or extension before making potential changes, customers and partners must still manually configure their application or extension if they are affected. That extra configuration step must be done by the application owner. See our full guide for detailed steps.

If your application or extension uses these components and SAP cookies only, this configuration step is sufficient to make your application or extension ready.

If your solution or extension uses either Approuter or SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service, and sets its own cookies, you must fully implement CHIPS and SAA. Our guide can help you decide what solution is the best for your scenario.

Below is a brief recap of how both solutions work.

 

Cookies Having Independent State (CHIPS)

Partitioning cookies allows an embedded application to access cookies in its own third-party context. This solution is mostly suitable if the cookies are used for information that is only relevant in their respective context, e.g session cookies, application state, caches, etc…

With cookie partitioning, a third-party service that sets a cookie when embedded in one top-level site cannot access that same cookie when the service is embedded in other top-level sites.

 

Storage Access API (SAA)

Storage Access API is a JavaScript API that allows iFrames to request storage access permissions when access would otherwise be denied by your browser settings. It also enables access to cookies in a third-party context the same way you would access them in a first-party context.

Both solutions might require additional configuration depending on your application scenario. Check our full dedicated guide for more information.

 

Further Information

This is the final part of our blog series covering the third-party cookie deprecation:

  • Are You Affected by the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation? 
  • How to test your solution for breakage? Full testing guide is now available: Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation .
  • How to handle deprecation trial tokens? Full deployment guide is now available: Getting and Applying a Deprecation Trial Token for the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation
  • How to prepare your application for the blockage of third-party cookies. Full implementation guide in now available: Implementing a Permanent Solution for the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation

entry.title: SAPPHIRE 2024 – My impressions and vision on what is ahead for us
entry.content:
With SAPPHIRE 2024 behind us, I here take the opportunity to collect my impressions and highlights of the event. Even if subtle, this year marks a very important milestone for SAP, its very first “Corporate GenAI positioning and strategy”. Sure GenAI Hub has been there for a little while but 2024 is the year where things start to materialize, really. The GenAI train (industry) rolls at 200Km/h, will SAP be able to act fast? It seems SAP is on the right direction but it won’t be easy and possibly some clarification and focus is required. I will comment along the list of highlights.

 

 

Key Points from SAP Sapphire Announcement

 

SAP Joule across SAP Cloud products

   - SAP has announced that Joule will be available across SAP Cloud products.

My take:

This is a coherent approach as we need a single user experience across multiple SAP solutions. Also, some capabilities should be cross-system. So I believe this is a good move.

Also, it reinforces the strategy of SAP of driving innovation in the cloud. Rise and Grow are clearly center to this discussion.

We continue to follow SAP’s pace on delivering on that promise. Joule was announced last year and it is becoming a reality now. The pace of innovation around GenAI is very fast. Will SAP be able to follow that pace? I think it can, with the help of partners. Joule needs to be open. Open for extensions and open to consume services from other platforms. (more on that later)

 

 

SAP Joule for consultants and Joule for ABAP Developers

   - SAP has announced that Joule be delivered with 2 new capabilities, Joule for Consultants and Joule for ABAP Developers.

The first, Joule for Consultants, will be one that has grounding (knowledge base) around help.sap.com, training documentation and a lot of more SAP related content. So this is quite promising. Don’t remember seeing a mention on availability.

The Joule for ABAP Developers however is one that needs more ground work from SAP as it requires a fine-tuned LLM. I’ve heard it is scheduled to be delivered in Q4’24 (so we should expect access in Q1 2025)

My take:

These are amongst the best announcements for me at SAPPHIRE. The industry is rely needing this. As we work in this field everyday, it is clear that there are areas that we need SAP to drive. That is:

  1. SAP Groundings (Knowledge Bases)
  2. ABAP Model

I go even one step forward, I believe SAP should make these groundings available (by subscription if needed be) so we can build our GenAI solutions leveraging them. A lot of players in the industry are doing exactly that and it makes no sense to reinvent the wheel.

 

Advancements in AI

   - SAP is enhancing its Business AI capabilities, providing users with advanced tools and insights to improve outcomes and decision-making.

My take:

Two important moves from SAP have been made in recent years, and that will make Business AI adoption better.

1 – Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management is the new APP that consolidates all Business AI innovations inside S/4. It makes easy to browse and navigate.

2 – Pricing around innovations has moved to a AI-UNITS consumption model. As we focus on the midmarket, where volumes are smaller than large enterprise, making business cases have just been made simpler.

 

 

 

New Integrations and Partnerships

   - SAP is expanding its collaborations with technology leaders like AWS, Microsoft, Google Cloud, Meta, Mistral AI, and NVIDIA to push the boundaries of what generative AI can achieve at an enterprise level.

My take:

This are great news but it will all come down to tangible improvements and innovations. We will need to continue following to make sure when this will be made available. Some are more immediate like the LLM models of Meta and Mistral AI. Nvidia’s partnership is a promising one but we need to wait until it is delivered (referring here to Joule’s capabilities and Joule for Developers.

 

 

 

GenAI Hub

   - The SAP Business Technology Platform is incorporating large language models from AWS, Meta, and Mistral AI into its generative AI hub.

   - This integration simplifies the development of generative AI use cases for SAP applications, facilitating seamless access to information and improved automation.

 

My take:

GenAI Hub is needed. We need SAP to deliver on this. We need to model and build GenAI capabilities in BTP development. I saw the early preview of the new release and it is very promising. It will deliver guardrails capabilities, Grounding management, more LLM availability, etc

But while I believe SAP needs to play in this field, providing technical tooling and capabilities, it shouldn’t be done with a goal of delivering the best (and sole) genAI platform around SAP. There are so many alternatives out there that it will be impossible for SAP to lead (and I honestly think it should not even try to). The ideal situation is one that this platform is open. It needs to be able to consume features from other platforms and be able to be consumed by other platforms. It needs to be able to handle local LLMs and consume already purchased hybrid LLMs (many companies have already contracts with Azure for OpenAI, same for other hyperscalers like AWS)

Where I am confident SAP can win is on its data. Let me be more precise:

  1. SAP needs to make available grounding (Knowledge base) around its SAP Documentation (Help, training etc) - I see it is built (or in the works, but when will it be made available for us to use in our GenAI innovations?)
  2. SAP needs to provide an ABAP fine-tuned LLM (Same as above, when?)
  3. SAP should provide tools to generate knowledge bases on customer’s own data as it runs all data on HANA (Figure out an auto vectorize S/4 content for example)
  4. AND OF COURSE – the BusinessAI. As SAP is the leader in the ERP space, it is best positioned to lead AI innovation would business processes. SAP is moving in that direction with its impressive AI content and roadmap, but relying on partners will be the only way to scale.

entry.title: Stock Room Management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear all,

SAP WM will disappear after 2027 in the S/4HANA world.

Stock Room Management is part of SAP WM but with less functionalities. It has the same coding and the same tables like SAP WM.

Furthermore Stock Room Management have the same transactions like SAP WM.

But how I make the difference in the system that I use Stock Room Management and not SAP WM?

Is there a customizing setting that I choose Stock Room Management and not SAP WM?

How I make the difference in the system?

Thanks a lot.

BR,

Christian

Read this post from @anwilk and register now! 

*Please note that this event entry is a summarized version of the original event and your registration may be required by the organizer shared on the original event website. RSVP accuracy here is without guarantee and does not constitute registration. However, if you want to earn an SAP Community badge, please leave your RSVP here as well.

is there a way to change WBS element ( as these are greyed ) ?

thanks a lot.

Romeo.

entry.title: Re: Our Way into the Clean Core
entry.content: Thanks for a really interesting read from a number of perspectives, to see how an SAP partner approaches this challenge, and learning how you are planning to gradually phase to a clean core, this can also become a roadmap for SAP customers too.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Check Clearing Date
entry.content:
Hello, 

As answered before for the same question. 

To retrieve the "Check Clearing Date" field in a Custom CDS View in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can look into the standard CDS View FINS_ACDOCA.

The "ABLAD" (Clearing Date) field in this CDS View represents the "Check Clearing Date" that you are looking for. You can include this field in your custom payment report by enhancing the standard CDS View or creating a new custom CDS View based on FINS_ACDOCA.

I hope this helps. Good luck with your report customization!

 

BR
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi from SAP in Melbourne Australia. I started working with SAP in the late 90s on a Y2K project and have never looked back. I enjoy using innovation to improve business processes and have had the opportunity to work around the world. At the moment I’m focused on SAP Business Network and look forward to being part of the community

entry.title: Transition to SAP Cloud ALM – We’ve Got the Solution
entry.content:
After being a mainstay of SAP’s strategy for multiple decades, mainstream maintenance for SAP Solution Manager is due to expire at the end of 2027. While this may seem like a “tomorrow problem” at first glance, it’s safe to say this isn’t a long time in the world of business software. For users of this well-established product, SAP therefore recommends transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM well before this date.

The good news is that customers are not alone, SAP is on hand to provide the support and tools needed to prepare and execute this transition. At the upcoming SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 in Mannheim, Germany (Sep 24-27), there will be a dedicated track of sessions outlining exactly what you need to consider, and how to go about it. To give you a small taste, expect to hear all about Solution Documentation, Test Management, Change Request Management, and first-hand experiences from SAP customers. Read on to find out more!

Functional overview of our SAP solutions

To guide customers through the transition and make it as smooth as possible, SAP is breaking it down into distinct stages for our customers, primarily zooming in on the first two columns shown below:

As you’ll see, already today customers can – and should – consider adopting SAP Cloud ALM for operations and SAP Cloud ALM for service. Not only will this minimize their update efforts, crucially it will simplify their monitoring tasks and ease their use and consumption. Going forward, customers can then adopt SAP Cloud ALM for implementation at their own pace as functionality is added progressively throughout this year and beyond.

Beyond that, customers may also consider adding SAP Focused Run if they need to monitor large landscapes or high volumes of data.

 

Kicking off the transition with the SAP Readiness Check

If you look at the overview graphic below, you’ll see the journey starts with the SAP Readiness Check. The latest version of the SAP Readiness Check was just released on June 12, 2024, so now is the ideal starting point to work out what your current usage is, and how the transition can benefit you even further.

High-level overview of the transition to SAP Cloud ALM

SAP will be providing various recommendations depending on what customers are using their SAP Solution Manager for. The last thing anyone wants is any nasty surprises, so customers must ensure they read all the available information carefully to assess their own situations. If you’re still not sure where to start, the Transition section on the SAP Support Portal is a great place to begin. Also keep your eyes open for an SAP Cloud ALM transition roadmap based on SAP Activate Methodology to guide our customers through the transition.

 

Migrating Data with the Selective Data Transfer

The plan is for our customers to use the Selective Data Transfer (SDT) to migrate their relevant data from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM. Once the right tools are available, they should then be able to define and tailor their scope flexibly, ensuring that their important data is retained in their future setup. Customers will be able to use the SDT for certain data as soon as it is available and then going forward, they stand to benefit from the full potential offered by the SDT. At the same time, it’s also worth remembering that monitoring and operations capabilities require no data migration at all.

Once available, the plan is that SAP Solution Manager customers on SP19 will be able to call up the SDT and even the Transition section of the SAP Support Portal directly from within their launchpad (customers on certain earlier SPs should also be able to access the SDT via a transaction instead of the launchpad).

Handling Change Control Management

Regarding the Change Request Management (aka. ChaRM) needs of our customers, note that data migration is not provided. SAP’s advice here is to follow the SAP Cloud ALM roadmap until everything you need is there, while already familiarizing yourselves with Deployment Management in SAP Cloud ALM. And for any customers not currently using ChaRM at all, this may be the ideal opportunity to improve their software logistics and stabilize their environment by using the Deployment Management features offered by SAP Cloud ALM. 

 

Closing Words

As your main take-away, just keep in mind that this transition needs to be tackled sooner rather than later, and that SAP is on hand to support customers every step of the way with the following tools and guidance:

Find out more at the SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 this September in Mannheim, so sign up today to join us and other top experts. Feel free to share this within your community network and get involved. Thanks as always for your time!

entry.title: Oracle tables not available in BW/4HANA(under HANA_SDA option) post connection.

entry.title: Remove the ability to delete a purchasing order in Fiori App Manage Material Coverage
entry.content:
in S/4 public cloud - In the tile “Monitor Material Coverage – individual/net segment” if I click on “Stock availability” then “Check Material Coverage” I switch to another tile called “Manage Material Coverage”.

Here, it’s possible to delete a purchasing order. We would like to remove this possibility.

in S/4 PUBLIC Cloud the catalog which allow to access to the tule “Monitor material coverage – Net/Individual” and “Manage Material Coverage” is :

  • SAP_SCM_BC_MRPCOCKPIT_MC

Do you think it's possible to remove the ability to delete a purchasing order with restriction while retaining access to the tile and all other features?

SAP Fiori Cloud

entry.title: Re: The ABAP Detective Investigates Noise on the Wire
entry.content:
(continuing the investigation into noise on the wire)

With symptoms noted previously, I speculated on possible root causes, and how to decide which detective steps to take, in which order. Of the basic network challenges, one phrase stuck in my mind: "It's DNS. It's always DNS." Using that as a possible smoking gun, I switched the server/agents connections from FQDN (fully qualified domain name) to IP address (which Zabbix allows nicely).

No change to the issue.

Similarly, agent connectivity for this suite has "active" and not active configurations. Also easy to change, so that was fix try #2. No effect.

Third search direction: assume there is a traffic contention issue and turn off everything but the minimal sandbox I set up that duplicated the issue. Two ways I could do this: gradual, by disabling agents one-by-one, or in larger batches, or just shut off the "production" servers running on the local network. That was brute force, which in a shared environment would require planning and permissions, but for a single user landscape, a risk worth taking (minimal business impact).

A few minutes later, I logged the last network anomaly message:

  4857:20240616:231310.765 resuming Zabbix agent checks on host "nbhp": connection restored

An early lesson I learned in the "deploy an agent and poll for data" matrix was "be patient" as the re-sample cycle wheels turned and the root-cause-analysis algorithms build up their caches. After several cycles when agent connections had dropped before, no messages meant success (or at the least, progress).

Like earlier, I went from logs to graphics and viewed the results. First view, looking good!

Noise fix image 1

Two agents, now both at 100% availability. Here patience is required. Let's wait a few more cycles and check that no backwash happened.

Noise fix image 2

Yes, continued lack of noise and possible data loss. Now what? To verify this change was as effective as it seemed, add back in the shut-down systems. Ah, another connection failure after the restart.

Noise fix image 3

Definite progress finding the likely root cause, though not the full details (yet). The next morning, with only one noise-inducer running (out of 3 running prior), the change to the system behavior is evident in the charts.

Noise fix image 4

Each red or green spike to the left of the arrow (when systems shut off) is a failure, to communicate. To the right of the arrow are many fewer drops since.

Case closed? Not quite yet, as we have a suspect. Just need a confession, and a motive.

<to be continued!>

entry.title: Need BW, BO, HANA , Memory and Run time statistics
entry.content:
Please let me know how to get Time and date, User name, Process type(process chain, BW Report, BO Report, HANA Views ), Memory consumed, Duration it took using HANA SQL and any other method.

We need to immediately understand which PC OR Bex Report, or HANA View is causing performance issues.


Regards,
Naiduz

entry.title: Streams in RAP (managed BO), how to keep stream content in connected DMS rather than in database
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have implemented a SAP RAP based app (managed scenario) that allows for uploading of attachments to some datamodel defined in a CDS Data Model to which the attachment entity is a composition child. As per RAP Solution Design the file content (Stream) get's stored in the SAP Database in a field of the the data base table stated in 'draft table' (while in draft state) and 'persistent table' (after draft becomes active entity). 

Our requirement is to store the stream content at time of draft action active and store it in the Document Management System connected to the backend instead of in the persistent table. The problem: when the stream ist meant to be downloaded from the Fiori Elements app the 'Select' statement executed in CL_SADL_SQL_EXECUTOR-> SELECT assumes the data to in the persistent table and there is no way I could find to supplement the stream data from the connected Document Management System instead.

Did you come accross this requirement in the context of managed RAP BOs using streams? How did you solve? I'd appreciate your insights.

Thanks,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: S4CLOUD : Adobe Form - Purchase Order Item Text not D...
entry.content:
Hello

 It seems like there might be an issue with the binding or the form configuration. Have you checked the binding properties to ensure that it is correctly linked to the PO item text? It's also important to verify if the PO item text data is being correctly fetched from the backend system.

If the binding and data fetching are all set up correctly, then you may need to review the form configuration to ensure that the PO item text is included in the output. Sometimes, certain fields may be hidden or incorrectly configured in the form layout.

some steps you can follow to debug and analyze the issue with the custom form for the purchase order in S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2402:

1. Check the binding: Start by checking the binding of the PurchaseOrderHeaderTextElement to the PO item text. Ensure that the binding is correctly configured and that it is referencing the correct field in the data source.

2. Check data source: Verify that the data source for the PO item text is correctly maintained and that the relevant data is available for the form output. You can do this by checking the data source in the backend system or using debugging tools provided by S/4 HANA.

3. Test in a different context: Try using the PurchaseOrderHeaderTextElement in a different context or form to see if the issue is specific to the custom form or if it is a broader data-related problem.

4. Debugging tools: Utilize the debugging tools provided by S/4 HANA, such as the Built-in debugger or the SAP Fiori Elements app to inspect the data and the form output. This can help you identify any issues in the data or the form configuration.

Let me know if this help.

 

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: And Next on Stage is...
entry.content:
The SAP Sapphire event in Orlando has just wrapped up, marking the end of a fantastic week filled with innovation, insightful sessions, and meaningful networking opportunities. Attendees experienced a showcase of SAP’s latest advancements and strategies, making it a memorable and impactful event. As the excitement from Sapphire continues to resonate, anticipation builds for the next installment of Discover SAP YouTube series, set to premiere on June 19th with Christian Freytag, Chief Data and Technology Officer at SAP IT. Learn how our digital IT organization supports our business as we move to cloud-based business models and evolve our enterprise architecture strategy.

The first session with the Senior Director of SAP Global Experience Technology at SAP IT, Oliver Gutzeit launched just a few weeks ago and in case you missed it, you can catch the replay on the SAP YouTube Channel. Looking ahead, the Discover SAP series has two more exciting sessions lined up for July 2024 hosted by one of the Cloud Solution Architects in the Platform Center of Excellence, Alexandra Hofmann. These will give some insights into how we enable all SAP developers with foundational governance within SAP Business Technology Platform and how we implement SAP Build solutions to accelerate business. These and the following sessions in fall of 2024 will delve further into practical applications, provide some hands-on demos on how SAP uses its own solutions and give a glimpse into innovative solutions, ensuring that the momentum from Sapphire continues throughout the whole year Stay tuned for these insightful episodes that will keep the SAP community engaged and informed.

 The “Discover SAP” series is a joint event by SAP’s IT and SAP Global User Groups Organization. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series gives the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into the inner workings of the digital landscape. We shine a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. We leverage the power of storytelling and show how SAP runs. 

Visit the Discover SAP webpage, register for the series, join the sessions, and come along for the ride. Discover SAP isn’t just a show; it's an experience. So, let’s dive into the world of SAP together!

entry.title: DataSphere - Replication Flow : Enabling the ABAP CDS Extractor
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere Replication Flow:

SAP Datasphere has introduced a new feature, 'Replication Flows’ which allows for the process of copying multiple tables/extractors from one source to another, offering a fast and seamless experience in data management. For detailed insights into replication flows and their functionalities, please refer below link

Configure Your Replication Flow | SAP Help Portal

Scenario –

In the current landscape, SAP Data Services is used for moving the SAP ECC Data to Azure Data Lake. The customer was moving to S4HANA and wanted to use SAP DataSphere for analytics and loading data to Azure Data Lake.

Datasphere is the best tool for analytics and data warehousing. After technical discussion , SAP Datasphere replication flow was finalized to move the data from S4HANA to Azure Data Lake. We did do the POC using the Datasphere trial version, and all looked fine.

Creating Connection in Datasphere:

Connection in Datasphere

Issue: In Datasphere replication flow, extractor enabled ABAP CDS view were not available

Solution:

The extractor enabled ABAP CDS views were not visible when replication flow was selected for development. These views were available if we select the option for Import Entities in Data Builder. I checked lot of forums and raised a query.

SAP Community - Query

The issue was also raised in SAP Customer Influence:

SAP Customer Influence

The below SAP note had to be implemented to resolve the issue. We raised the issue with SAP as well and they also asked us to apply the SAP Note. Please go through the note as it talks about implementing 60+ SAP Notes.

3130827 - SAP Data Intelligence - ABAP Integration - SAP S/4HANA 2022

After the note implementation the issue was resolved.

I will come up with a step-by-step blog on loading data from S4HANA to Azure Data Lake using Datasphere replication flow. Currently working on it 😊

Helpful link:

3297105 - Important considerations for SAP Datasphere Replication Flows

3471218 - Datasphere - CDS View list in Data Flow and Replication Flow

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom BO with upload function like 'Import...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes it is possible, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,

Option 1 via In-app extensibility:

It is possible to create custom business objects with upload functions using Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here are the steps to guide you:

1. Go to the Extensibility Pane in the SAP Fiori launchpad and select the Custom Fields and Logic app.

2. Click on the "New" button to create a new custom business object.

3. Enter the details for the new business object, such as the name, description, and relevant fields.

4. Once the business object is created, you can add fields to it and define the upload function by adding an attachment field.

5. Use the "Add Field" button to add the attachment field to the business object.

6. Define the properties and settings for the attachment field, such as allowing multiple file uploads and setting the file size limits.

7. Save your changes and activate the custom business object.

8. Test the upload function by creating a new record for the custom business object and attaching files to it.

This way you can create a custom business object with an upload function similar to the Import Supplier Invoices App in S/4HANA Cloud. This will allow you to extend the functionality of your system and meet your specific business needs.

Option 2 via Developer Extensibility:

follow these steps to create a custom business object with an upload function using Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

Step 1: Define the custom business object
- Navigate to the Extensibility app in SAP Fiori launchpad.
- Select "Custom Fields and Logic" and then click on "Custom Business Objects".
- Click on "Create" and define the custom business object by specifying its name, description, and other relevant details.

Step 2: Add attributes to the custom business object
- Once the custom business object is created, click on "Add" under the "Fields and Logic" tab.
- Add the required attributes for the custom business object. For example, if you want to create an upload function for supplier invoices, you can add attributes such as Invoice Number, Invoice Date, Supplier Name, Amount, etc.

Step 3: Define the upload function
- Under the "Actions" tab, click on "Add" to define the upload function.
- Specify the name and details of the upload function. For example, you can name it "Upload Invoices" and provide a description.

Step 4: Implement the upload logic
- Once the upload function is defined, you can implement the logic for uploading supplier invoices. This can be done using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) or directly in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system using relevant APIs and services.

Step 5: Test and deploy the custom business object
- After implementing the upload logic, thoroughly test the custom business object to ensure it functions as expected.
- Once validated, deploy the custom business object to make it available for use in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

Keep in mind that the exact implementation may vary based on specific requirements and system configurations. It is recommended to refer to the official SAP documentation and consult with SAP experts for a more detailed and tailored approach.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy xsuaa service with xs-security.json
entry.content: { "xsappname": "ccstemplate", "tenant-mode": "dedicated", "scopes": [ { "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Admin", "description": "Admin" } ], "attributes": [], "role-templates": [ { "name": "Admin", "description": "generated", "scope-references": [ "$XSAPPNAME.Admin" ], "attribute-references": [] } ], "oauth2-configuration": { "credential-types": [ "binding-secret", "x509" ], "redirect-uris": [ "http://localhost:5000/**", "https://*.*.hana.ondemand.com/**", "https://*.launchpad.cfapps.*.hana.ondemand.com/**" ] } }

entry.title: Cross-Origin Theming for Classic UIs
entry.content:
Since December '23, the UI theme designer on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, allows to Add Cross Origin Preview Pages to the UI theme designer, as theming previews. Those applications have to implement a special interface, described in the tutorial Add A Cross-Origin Preview for the UI Theme Designer, and will then display theme changes in real time.

Today I'm happy to announce that, starting with Cumulative Patch for UR-c2 2406, applications built with Classic UIs can be used as cross-origin theming previews, i.e. applications based on SAP GUI for HTML, or based on Web Dynpro ABAP. UR-c2 implements the described interface and, with that, displays theme changes immediately.

This theme change preview is even faster than using regular UR c2 preview applications that are part of UI theme designer. That is because they only depend on a very small subset of theming parameters (the theming-base-content). So the majority of theming happens at runtime in the browser, whereas for regular UR c2 previews the whole CSS has to be compiled for every theme change.

However, due to the nature of cross-origin embedded applications, the control picker of the Expert panel does not work. That is because the control picker has to analyze the picked element, and, since the application is embedded cross-origin, the browser does not allow this analysis for security reasons.

So: follow SAP Note 2500800 Cumulative Patches for Unified Rendering (UR-c2 and Lightspeed) to install the latest UR-c2 for your Classic UI backend, and start using your Classic UI applications as theming previews today! Then let me know in the comments how that went.

entry.title: Re: Guide in a needed middleware integration with SAP
entry.content: Thank you very much @Ulrich_Schmidt, sure I'll be checking the links contents following your indications. Once I can confirm it suits our needs, I'll be back checking the answer as Solution.

entry.title: Automatic account assignement on Purchase Order Items ( SAp ByD )
entry.content:
Hello ByD Experts,

Is it possible to automatically enter the right GL account for purchase order items.
For exemple, if company A buys an item from Company B, the GL account assignement will be done automatically depending if the item is non-stock or if it is a material or a service.

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP Trial installation stalling on unbuntu
entry.content: Ubuntu works also, no problem

entry.title: Re: Is PO Item Text output within form can be controlled...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes it is possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Sytem.

1. Log in to your S4 HANA public cloud system and navigate to the Purchase Order application.

2. Go to the "Form Template Maintenance" app.

3. Select the relevant form template for the purchase order.

4. In the form template, locate the section where the purchase order item text is displayed.

5. Click on the "More" button or right-click on the section to access the settings for that specific field.

6. Look for an option to set restrictions based on the purchase organization.

7. If there is an option available, select the appropriate purchase organization and set the restriction to not allow printing for that organization.

8. Save the changes to the form template.

9. Test the restriction by creating a purchase order for the specified purchase organization and attempting to print the purchase order item text.

10. If the restriction is working as expected, the printing of the purchase order item text will be restricted for the specified purchase organization.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: Material master upload

Hope this message finds you hale and healthy!

Poongodi Elangovan.

entry.title: Unable to suppress group footer section based on condition
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have created one SAP crystal report. In this report I have created one group based on row headers. There are 4 row headers so 4 sections are created.(suppose the sections are A,B,C,D)

I have suppressed details section.

In this main report I have created 4 sub reports. I have enter this reports in group footers. I want to print this sub report for each row header. example. 1st sub report should print for A row header , 2nd sub report should print for B row header.

I am trying to achieve this but the issue is all the 4 reports are printing for all 4 row headers.

Could you please help on this issue ?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - Customization for Messages without Integration Workflow Configuration
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

For the message “Inspection Results Recording” there is no Workflow Configuration and we have following problem:

These are the CI Artifacts triggered from this Message.

We need to update the Bapi Service Processor (BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS) to send the SFC as parameter EXT_NO (Single-Unit Number for the Unit to Be Inspected).

What is the best way to make this kind of customization?

Another question: Is it possible to trigger another IFlow for this message? (without using an Automatic Trigger/Production Process Designer)

Thanks in advance

Gabriela

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori UI Adaptation: Hide 'Standard' View
entry.content:
Hello, 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can achieve this requirement by using the Manage Variants functionality. Here's  how to prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table:

1. Navigate to the app where the smart table object is located.

2. Click on the 'Variant Management' button on the toolbar of the smart table. This will open the Manage Variants dialog.

3. In the Manage Variants dialog, select the Custom variant that you have created for the Roles table.

4. Click on the 'Set as Default' button to set the Custom variant as the default variant for the smart table.

5. Next, click on the 'Authorization' tab in the Manage Variants dialog. Here, you can restrict the access to the Standard variant for specific roles or users. You can remove the authorization for the Standard variant for all users or specific roles.

6. Once you have set the Custom variant as the default and removed the authorization for the Standard variant, click on 'Save' to apply the changes.

this way you can prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table in the Fiori app. This will ensure that the fields hidden in the Custom variant will not be accessible to the user.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: SAP SCREEN PERSONAS - SAP EWM WAREHOUSE MONITOR
entry.content:
Hello

Yes, it is possible to disable a button within the Warehouse Monitor app in S/4 HANA Public Cloud based on movement type and applicable for certain users.

To do this, you would need to use the SAP Fiori UI adaptation tool to customize the Warehouse Monitor app. You can use the UI adaptation tool to configure the visibility and behavior of UI elements, such as buttons, based on certain conditions.

First, you would need to identify the specific movement types and users for which you want to disable the button. Then, using the UI adaptation tool, you can create a rule that checks the movement type and user information, and based on that rule, you can disable the button for the specified conditions.

The UI adaptation tool provides a user-friendly interface for making these customizations without the need for extensive coding. Once the rule is created and applied, the button will be disabled as per your specified conditions.

It's important to note that making these customizations may require appropriate authorizations and access to the UI adaptation tool. 

BR
Harish Mangtani
